title: Give me a differential diagnosis for extrapulmonary tuberculosis in tabulated format.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Give me a differential diagnosis for extrapulmonary tuberculosis in tabulated format.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
| No.  | Differential Diagnosis  | Key Features                                                                                       |
|-----|------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1.  | Lymphadenitis          | Swollen, tender lymph nodes; common in children; often caused by bacterial or viral infections      |
| 2.  | Sarcoidosis            | Noncaseating granulomas; commonly affects the lungs, lymph nodes, and skin; may show elevated ACE levels |
| 3.  | Brucellosis            | Flu-like symptoms; chronic fatigue; possible granulomas in organs; commonly transmitted from animals      |
| 4.  | Cat-scratch disease    | Swollen lymph nodes, skin lesions at the site of a cat scratch; caused by Bartonella henselae        |
| 5.  | Actinomycosis          | Chronic infection with suppurative abscesses and draining sinuses; caused by Actinomyces species     |
| 6.  | Fungal infections      | Granulomatous lesions; possible dissemination in immunocompromised patients; e.g., histoplasmosis, coccidioidomycosis |
| 7.  | Nocardiosis            | Suppurative and granulomatous lesions; may present as pulmonary, cutaneous or systemic infection      |
| 8.  | Toxoplasmosis          | Enlarged lymph nodes; commonly associated with exposure to cats; may cause encephalitis in an immunocompromised patient |
| 9.  | Lymphoma               | Malignant lymphoid cells leading to enlarged, painless lymph nodes; may be associated with B symptoms (fever, weight loss, night sweats) |
| 10. | Metastatic cancer      | Lymphadenopathy in the setting of known primary malignancy or unknown primary and presence of multiple involved lymph nodes |

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
| Disease/Condition | Symptoms | Diagnostic Tests | Treatment |

Follow the format above and include only the 3 most common disease.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
| Disease/Condition      | Symptoms                                               | Diagnostic Tests                                         | Treatment                                     |
|------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Lymphadenitis          | Swollen, tender lymph nodes                            | Physical examination, blood tests, imaging, possible biopsy | Antibiotics, pain management, drainage of abscesses if needed |
| Sarcoidosis            | Fatigue, shortness of breath, enlarged lymph nodes, skin rashes, joint pain | Chest X-ray, blood tests (including ACE levels), bronchoscopy with biopsy, possibly PET scan         | Corticosteroids, immunosuppressive medications, monitoring disease progression    |
| Extrapulmonary Tuberculosis | Lymph node swelling, fever, weight loss, night sweats, fatigue | Tuberculin skin test, blood tests, imaging (e.g., CT scan), biopsy for histopathology and culture | Antitubercular medications (e.g., isoniazid, rifampin, pyrazinamide, and ethambutol) for a prolonged duration (at least 6 months) |
